skill.name: firecrawl-build-onboarding
skill.description: Get Firecrawl credentials and SDK setup into a project. Use when an application needs `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`, when an agent should add Firecrawl to `.env`, when the user wants to authenticate Firecrawl for app code, or when choosing the first SDK and docs for a new Firecrawl integration. This skill includes its own browser auth flow, so it does not depend on the website onboarding skill.
skill.summary: Get Firecrawl API credentials and SDK setup into a project for app integration.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 76 potential security issues. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. External commands use hardcoded system tools for PKCE credential generation. Network calls target only the legitimate Firecrawl API (firecrawl.dev). Environment variable access is intentional for FIRECRAWL_API_KEY setup. SHA256 cryptographic operations are correct PKCE implementation, not weak crypto. The skill is official Firecrawl onboarding from the firecrawl/skills repository.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Patterns (All False Positives)
dangerous_patterns.description: 35 external command executions flagged. All are hardcoded system commands (openssl, tr, npm, pip) for legitimate credential setup. No command injection vectors exist. The skill generates PKCE parameters and installs SDKs using standard tooling with no user input passed to shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Access to Firecrawl API (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: 13 network access points all target the legitimate Firecrawl API service (firecrawl.dev). This is expected behavior for a Firecrawl integration skill. No data exfiltration to external servers detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Access for Credential Setup (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: 13 env_access detections are all intentional FIRECRAWL_API_KEY environment variable access for legitimate credential setup. No malicious credential harvesting.

dangerous_patterns.title: SHA256 PKCE Implementation (False Positive - Not Weak Crypto)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged SHA256 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is incorrect. SHA256 is the current recommended algorithm for PKCE in OAuth 2.0 (RFC 7636). The skill implements correct PKCE flow for secure browser-based authentication.

content.user_title: Set up Firecrawl API credentials and SDK in your project
content.value_statement: Developers integrating Firecrawl face the challenge of proper API authentication and SDK setup. This skill automates the browser auth flow, PKCE credential generation, SDK installation, and environment configuration so you can start making Firecrawl API calls in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Run browser-based OAuth PKCE authentication flow to obtain FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
Generate secure cryptographic parameters (code verifier, code challenge) using OpenSSL
Install Firecrawl SDK for Node.js or Python with one command
Save API credentials to .env file with proper dotenv configuration
Verify API key works by running a test request against the Firecrawl API
Guide selection of the right Firecrawl endpoint based on use case
limitations: Does not include live web scraping or crawling capabilities
Cannot automate browser interactions beyond the initial auth flow
Requires human browser interaction for the OAuth authorization step
Only supports Node.js, Python, Rust, Java, Elixir, and REST for SDK integration

use_cases.title: New project needs Firecrawl web scraping
use_cases.description: You are starting a new project and want to add web scraping capabilities using Firecrawl. The skill guides you through the complete setup: browser auth, API key configuration, SDK installation, and your first test request.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building new applications with web data extraction

use_cases.title: Adding Firecrawl to existing codebase
use_cases.description: You have an existing Node.js or Python project and need to integrate Firecrawl for scraping or search. The skill helps you add credentials to your existing .env setup and choose the right integration pattern.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted Firecrawl deployment setup
use_cases.description: Your organization runs Firecrawl on your own infrastructure. The skill configures both FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and FIRECRAWL_API_URL for self-hosted deployments, following your security requirements.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps teams managing self-hosted Firecrawl instances

prompt_templates.title: Basic Firecrawl setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firecrawl in my project. I need to scrape web pages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user with no existing Firecrawl credentials

prompt_templates.title: Existing API key provided
prompt_templates.prompt: I have my FIRECRAWL_API_KEY already. Help me install the Firecrawl SDK and make a test request to verify it works.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who already has API credentials

prompt_templates.title: Self-hosted configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Our team uses a self-hosted Firecrawl instance. Help me configure my project to use FIRECRAWL_API_URL for our internal deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enterprise user with custom Firecrawl deployment

prompt_templates.title: Complete integration project
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a content aggregation service. Help me set up Firecrawl, choose the right endpoints for scraping multiple URLs, and implement proper error handling and rate limiting.

output_examples.input: Set up Firecrawl in my Node.js project to scrape product pages
output_examples.output: Guide: 1) Run browser auth to get API key, 2) Add FIRECRAWL_API_KEY to .env, 3) npm install @mendable/firecrawl-js, 4) Create scrape function using docs.firecrawl.dev example, 5) Test with one URL

output_examples.input: I want to search for companies mentioned on a webpage
output_examples.output: Guide: 1) Verify API key present, 2) Use /search endpoint (not /scrape), 3) Pass the URL and entity extraction prompt, 4) Parse the structured response

output_examples.input: Help me configure for our self-hosted Firecrawl
output_examples.output: Guide: 1) Add both FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and FIRECRAWL_API_URL to .env, 2) Point to your internal instance URL, 3) Verify connectivity with a test request to your instance

best_practices: Keep your FIRECRAWL_API_KEY in environment variables or your platform secret manager, never in source code
After setup, run at least one test request to verify your API key works before writing production code
Use the narrowest Firecrawl endpoint that matches your use case: /scrape for pages, /search for discovery, /crawl for site-wide extraction
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in source files or commit them to version control
Do not use /crawl when /scrape or /search would suffice for your needs
Do not skip the smoke test step - verify your API key works before building features on top of it

faq.question: How do I get a Firecrawl API key?
faq.answer: The skill runs a browser-based OAuth flow that guides you through signing in or creating an account at firecrawl.dev. Once authorized, your API key is polled and saved to your project.

faq.question: What programming languages does Firecrawl support?
faq.answer: Firecrawl provides official SDKs for Node.js/TypeScript, Python, Rust, Java, and Elixir. You can also use REST/curl for any language that supports HTTP requests.

faq.question: What is the difference between /scrape, /search, and /crawl?
faq.answer: /scrape extracts content from a single URL. /search finds URLs matching a query. /crawl discovers and extracts content from an entire website domain.

faq.question: How do I use Firecrawl with a self-hosted instance?
faq.answer: Set both FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and FIRECRAWL_API_URL in your environment. The API_URL should point to your self-hosted Firecrawl deployment URL.

faq.question: Is it safe to commit my .env file to git?
faq.answer: No. Add .env to your .gitignore file. API keys and secrets should only exist in local environment files or secure secret managers provided by your hosting platform.

faq.question: Why is browser interaction required for auth?
faq.answer: Firecrawl uses OAuth PKCE flow for secure browser-based authentication. This requires you to sign in through the Firecrawl website to authorize API access, similar to how GitHub OAuth works.
